a212-1.description: d074 hook deletion MUST happen in next 2 B sessions. 3 consecutive audits flagged this.
a212-1.resolution: RESOLVED. B#575 (s1862) executed wq-899 (-7 A pre-hooks) and wq-904 (-5 R hooks) in a single session: 75→63 hooks. 12 hooks deleted — the largest single-session reduction in d074 history. Groups 1, 2, 3 all marked COMPLETE in HOOK_CONSOLIDATION_PLAN.md. wq-905 priority override worked. Subsequently, R#332 (s1861) and R#333 (s1863) absorbed Groups 6 and 7, with wq-906 (-3) and wq-907 (-1) pending for further reductions to 59.
a212-1.metric_after: Hook count 63. 12 deletions executed. Groups 1-3, 6-7 absorbed. 80% timeline elapsed. wq-906 (-3) and wq-907 (-1) pending for further 59.

active.title: Reduce hook system complexity
active.progress: MAJOR BREAKTHROUGH. Groups 1-3 complete (dispatchers built + old hooks deleted). Groups 6-7 absorbed. Hook count 75→63 (-12 actual deletions). wq-906 (-3) and wq-907 (-1) pending — would reach 59 (under ≤55 target requires Groups 4+5+8 for additional -9). Deliverable (4): 0 timing regressions. Timeline: 80% elapsed, ~50% of reduction target achieved.
risk_factors: wq-906 (-3) and wq-907 (-1) readily executable = 59 hooks
Reaching ≤55 requires Groups 4 (-3), 5 (-2), 8 (-4) — 3 more R+B pairs minimum
≤55 target mathematically tight but feasible if Groups 4+5 executed (59-3-2=54)
3 hooks still >300 lines — deliverable (3) blocked on inline extraction
active.strategic_value: HIGH — maintainability improvement demonstrable. 75→63 is already a meaningful result.
active.recommendation: Execute wq-906 and wq-907 in next B session to reach 59. Then prioritize Group 4 (R+B pair) to reach 56. Partial completion (59 hooks, 5/9 groups) is a reasonable outcome. Accept deliverables (2) and (3) as deferred.
